seed_coverage.answer_summary: Yes, the draft credit agreement contains multiple unauthorized fees, covenants, and definitions, as well as critical omissions. These include an unauthorized 0.50% SOFR floor on the Revolver (e803), unauthorized fee modifications in Section 2.06 (under-drafted arrangement, upfront, and agency fees) (e804, e805, e806), a reduced minimum assignment threshold for Revolving Commitments (e807), and unauthorized economic changes (25 bps TLB margin increase, tighter springing covenant trigger) that violate the 'no-flex' confirmation (e813, e866). It also lacks agreed-upon terms such as the MFN protection clause (e810), negative covenants (e811), Equity Cure provisions (e812), and key definitions like the 0.50% TLB SOFR floor (e415) and soft call protection (e741, e742).
seed_coverage.evidence_summary: The analytical state identifies numerous unauthorized deviations, fee discrepancies, and missing covenants/definitions between the draft credit agreement and the commitment documents.

criteria_coverage.answer_summary: The draft credit agreement fails to comply with the 'no-flex' confirmation. Despite David Sung's formal waiver of all market flex rights on June 2, 2025, the draft credit agreement (dated June 9, 2025) incorporates several unauthorized and unfavorable economic changes, including a 25 bps increase in the Term Loan B margin, a 50 bps floor on the Revolver, and a tighter springing covenant trigger.

criteria_coverage.answer_summary: The analytical state identifies several unauthorized fees and structural changes not contemplated in the commitment documents. These include an unauthorized 0.50% SOFR floor on the Revolving Credit Facility (representing up to $375,000/year in excess interest), significant fee discrepancies (under-drafted arrangement fee of $250k vs $6.125M, upfront fee of 1.00% vs 2.00%, and agency fee of $50k vs $150k), a reduced minimum assignment amount for Revolving Commitments ($1M vs $5M), the omission of the MFN protection clause, the complete absence of Article VI (Negative Covenants), and unauthorized economic changes (25 bps increase in TLB margin and tighter springing covenant trigger) that violate the 'no-flex' confirmation.
criteria_coverage.evidence_summary: Entries e803, e804, e805, e806, e807, e810, e811, e812, e813, and e866 detail these unauthorized fees and structural deviations between the draft credit agreement and the commitment documents.
